name: Zubeen Garg
description:
'''Zubeen Garg''' ({{IPA|as|zubin ɡaɹɡ}}; 18 November 1972 – 19 September 2025) was an Indian musician, composer, singer, songwriter who primarily worked and sang in the [[Assamese language|Assamese]], [[Bengali language|Bengali]], and [[Hindi]]-language films and music industries. He also sang in 40 other languages and [[dialect]]s, including [[Bishnupriya Manipuri language|Bishnupriya Manipuri]], [[Adi languages|Adi]], [[Boro language (India)|Boro]], [[Dimasa language|Dimasa]], [[English language|English]], [[Goalpariya dialects|Goalpariya]], [[Gujarati language|Gujarati]], [[Kannada]], [[Karbi language|Karbi]], [[Malayalam language|Malayalam]], [[Marathi language|Marathi]], [[Mising language|Mising]], [[Rabha language|Rabha]], [[Garo language|Garo]], [[Nepali language|Nepali]], [[Bhojpuri language|Bhojpuri]], [[Odia language|Odia]], [[Sadri language|Sadri]], [[Sanskrit]], [[Sindhi language|Sindhi]], [[Tamil language|Tamil]], [[Telugu language|Telugu]], [[Tiwa language (India)|Tiwa]] and [[Urdu language|Urdu]].

Garg was a [[multi-instrumentalist]] and played many instruments including [[anandalahari]], [[dhol]], [[dotara]], [[drum]]s, [[guitar]], [[harmonica]], [[Pump organ|harmonium]], [[mandolin]], [[Electronic keyboard|keyboard]], [[tabla]], and various [[percussion instrument]]s. He was regarded as one of the most influential musicians in Assam, and was reportedly the highest-paid singer in Assam. Over a career spanning 33 years, he recorded over 40,000 songs.{{efn|Credited to multiple sources which claim he recorded over 38,000 or 40,000 songs.<ref>{{cite web |title=Zubeen Garg: The Song That Stayed Home |url=https://www.indiatodayne.in/opinion/story/zubeen-garg-the-song-that-stayed-home-1283507-2025-09-27 |website=India Today NE |access-date=29 January 2026}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |title=Thousands of fans bid adieu to Zubeen at Guwahati stadium |url=https://www.thehindu.com/entertainment/music/singer-zubeen-gargs-body-arrives-at-guwahati-airport-thousands-gather-to-get-last-glimpse/article70076293.ece |access-date=23 September 2025 |work=[[The Hindu]] |date=21 September 2025 |language=en-IN |archive-date=21 September 2025 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20250921172437/https://www.thehindu.com/entertainment/music/singer-zubeen-gargs-body-arrives-at-guwahati-airport-thousands-gather-to-get-last-glimpse/article70076293.ece |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |last1=Deb |first1=Abhijit |title=From 'Anamika' to 'Ya Ali': Zubeen Garg, a man of many emotions, bids farewell |url=https://www.thestatesman.com/entertainment/from-anamika-to-ya-ali-zubeen-garg-a-man-of-many-emotions-bids-farewell-1503488612.html |access-date=23 September 2025 |work=[[The Statesman (India)|The Statesman]] |date=19 September 2025 |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":13">{{Citation |title=He is a young man who has preserved 35,000 songs of Zubeen Garg from 'Anamika' to 'Maya' | date=18 November 2022 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WdwhYtMoJuc |language=en |access-date=20 November 2022}}</ref><ref>{{Citation |title='After singing 35,000 songs, I don't need to take the Best Singer award' |date=13 March 2023 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dJWoPZknc0w |access-date=13 March 2023 |language=en |archive-date=13 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230313173241/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dJWoPZknc0w |url-status=live }}</ref>}}
